001package guiStaffHome;
002
003
004/*******
005 * <p> Title: ControllerStaffHome Class. </p>
006 * 
007 * <p> Description: The Java/FX-based Role 2 Home Page.  This class provides the controller
008 * actions basic on the user's use of the JavaFX GUI widgets defined by the View class.
009 * 
010 * This page is a stub for establish future roles for the application.
011 * 
012 * The class has been written assuming that the View or the Model are the only class methods that
013 * can invoke these methods.  This is why each has been declared at "protected".  Do not change any
014 * of these methods to public.</p>
015 * 
016 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
017 * 
018 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
019 * 
020 * @version 1.00                2025-08-17 Initial version
021 * @version 1.01                2025-09-16 Update Javadoc documentation *  
022 */
023
024public class ControllerStaffHome {
025        
026        /*-*******************************************************************************************
027
028        User Interface Actions for this page
029        
030        This controller is not a class that gets instantiated.  Rather, it is a collection of protected
031        static methods that can be called by the View (which is a singleton instantiated object) and 
032        the Model is often just a stub, or will be a singleton instantiated object.
033        
034         */
035
036        /**
037         * Default constructor is not used.
038         */
039        public ControllerStaffHome() {
040        }
041
042        /**********
043         * <p> Method: performUpdate() </p>
044         * 
045         * <p> Description: This method directs the user to the User Update Page so the user can change
046         * the user account attributes. </p>
047         * 
048         */
049        protected static void performUpdate () {
050                guiUserUpdate.ViewUserUpdate.displayUserUpdate(ViewStaffHome.theStage, ViewStaffHome.theUser);
051        }       
052
053        /**********
054         * <p> Method: performLogout() </p>
055         * 
056         * <p> Description: This method logs out the current user and proceeds to the normal login
057         * page where existing users can log in or potential new users with a invitation code can
058         * start the process of setting up an account. </p>
059         * 
060         */
061        protected static void performLogout() {
062                guiUserLogin.ViewUserLogin.displayUserLogin(ViewStaffHome.theStage);
063        }
064        
065        /**********
066         * <p> Method: performBackToLogin() </p>
067         * 
068         * <p> Description: This method returns to the login page without logging out. This allows
069         * another user to login while keeping the current session available.</p>
070         * 
071         */
072        protected static void performBackToLogin() {
073                guiUserLogin.ViewUserLogin.displayUserLogin(ViewStaffHome.theStage);
074        }
075
076        protected static void performOpenDiscussion() {
077                guiDiscussion.ViewDiscussionBoard.displayDiscussionBoard(ViewStaffHome.theStage,
078                                ViewStaffHome.theUser, ViewStaffHome.theRole);
079        }
080
081        protected static void performOpenTeacherGradebook() {
082                guiGradebook.ViewGradebook.displayGradebook(ViewStaffHome.theStage, ViewStaffHome.theUser);
083        }
084        
085        /**********
086         * <p> Method: performQuit() </p>
087         * 
088         * <p> Description: This method terminates the execution of the program.  It leaves the
089         * database in a state where the normal login page will be displayed when the application is
090         * restarted.</p>
091         * 
092         */     
093        protected static void performQuit() {
094                System.exit(0);
095        }
096}
